data.title: Venture Global cut at UBS after recent strong performance
data.description:
Venture Global (NYSE:VG) -2.3% in Thursday's trading as UBS downgraded shares to Neutral from Buy with an $18 price target, seeing limited upside left after shares have more than doubled in the last two months vs. a mere 6.6% gain in the S&P Energy sector and dramatically outperforming peer Cheniere.

Venture Global is enjoying positive momentum in its projects, UBS analyst Manav Gupta said: The company said recently it initiated full mobilization and started site work at CP2 LNG, its third liquefied natural gas export facility, which came shortly after CP2 received final approval and notices to proceed from the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission on the project, and its Plaquemines LNG project received FERC approval to introduce natural gas to its 12th liquefaction block, meaning the developer now has approval to flow gas to more than half of the full project, which will have nameplate LNG production capacity of 20M metric tons/year.

Gupta noted Venture Global's (NYSE:VG) modular approach to development, which allows for speed to market so the first LNG can be achieved in 2.5-3 years post FID compared to the traditional approach with long lead times of 5-7 years; the company starts marketing these cargoes during a long commissioning phase that can last up to 30 months, allowing it to capture the higher net spreads of global gas price vs. Henry Hub, which lowers overall project development costs, which are passed to its long-term customers.

data.title: Bitcoin is the new 'refuge' as U.S. debt grows exponentially, Coinbase CEO says
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is emerging as a "refuge" as concerns about ballooning U.S. debt and persistent inflation drive more investors to the cryptocurrency, Coinbase Global (NASDAQ:COIN) CEO Brian Armstrong said on Thursday.

"People are turning to bitcoin as a refuge from inflation and deficit spending," Armstrong said in a presentation at a Coinbase event.

"It's not a coincidence that we're seeing all-time high prices of bitcoin (BTC-USD)," he added. If the exponential growth in U.S. federal debt continues, "there's a very good chance bitcoin becomes" the next global reserve currency.

In the meantime, Armstrong also made several product announcements as crypto becomes more mainstream. He introduced a new Coinbase (NASDAQ:COIN) product for startups and medium-sized businesses; a new set of payment APIs that bridge the gap between stablecoins and fiat currencies; introducing derivatives trading and integrating decentralized exchange trading ("DEX"); and building a new consumer payment product.

Earlier at the same event, U.S. President Donald Trump's crypto czar David Sacks said "we're well on our way" to making the U.S. the global center for digital asset innovation, reversing a tough and unclear regulatory environment under the Biden administration.

data.title: Nurix drops after new early-stage trial data for lead asset
data.description:
Shares of Nurix Therapeutics (NASDAQ:NRIX) fell on Thursday after the company updated data from a Phase 1a/b clinical trial for its lead asset bexobrutideg (NX-5948) in patients with relapsed or refractory B-cell malignancies.

The data had been selected for a presentation at the ongoing European Hematology Association Congress.

According to Nurix (NASDAQ:NRIX), its Bruton’s tyrosine kinase degrader achieved an overall objective response rate of 80.9% among 47 patients with relapsed or refractory chronic lymphocytic leukemia.

Citing data from 19 patients, the company added that those with Waldenström Macroglobulinemia witnessed an overall objective response rate of 84.2%.

The analysis was based on a data cut on March 12 when the median follow-up period had approached nine months.

The study, designed to test oral bexobrutide doses ranging from 50 mg to 600 mg, indicated a well-tolerated safety profile for the drug with no safety signals, the company noted. Nurix (NASDAQ:NRIX) reaffirmed its plans to begin pivotal trials for bexobrutideg this year.

More on Nurix

  • Nurix Therapeutics: IRAK4 GS-6971 Isn't Only Protein Degrader With Big Potential
  • Nurix Therapeutics: A Story To Monitor
  • Nurix Therapeutics: Speculative Bet On BTK Degraders And Targeted Protein Modulation
  • Nurix gets FDA clearance to initiate trial for its anti-inflammatory drug candidate
  • Nurix Therapeutics GAAP EPS of -$0.67 beats by $0.11, revenue of $18.45M beats by $5.42M

data.title: Frito-Lay shutting down production at Southern California plant
data.description:
• Frito-Lay (NASDAQ:PEP) is shutting down production at its Rancho Cucamonga plant in Southern California after 50 years in operation.
  • Although no reason was given for the decision, Frito-Lay North America ("FLNA"), a division of PepsiCo (NASDAQ:PEP) reported an 11% decline in operating profit in Q4 2024, primarily reflecting operating cost increases and a decline in organic volume.
  • The company no longer strips out Frito-Lay North America financial results. The division is now included with Quaker Foods into a single unit, PepsiCo Foods North America ("PFNA"). 
  • Although the company will maintain its distribution, fleet, and warehouse operations at the Rancho Cucamonga plant, shutting down manufacturing is expected to result in the loss of “hundreds” of jobs, according to USA Today.
  • Because the Rancho Cucamonga plant is not registered with California’s Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification (WARN) database -- which requires employers to give 60 days’ notice before layoffs – the timing of the closure and the actual number of employees impacted is unknown.

data.title: CrowdStrike reports insider transaction worth ~$12M
data.description:
CrowdStrike Holdings (NASDAQ:CRWD) (NEOE:CRWD:CA) said in an SEC filing that President Michael Sentonas has recently sold stake worth $11.9M in the cybersecurity company.

Sentonas sold 25,000 shares of class A common stock at $477 per share.

data.title: Elon Musk's lawyers ask court to dismiss OpenAI countersuit
data.description:
Lawyers for Elon Musk have asked the court for a second time to dismiss a countersuit from OpenAI against him, according to a court filing.

News of the new court filing was first reported by The Information.

Musk had initially asked to have the lawsuit from the ChatGPT maker dismissed in May on the belief that his unsolicited $97.4B bid to buy OpenAI's assets — and complaints related to it — are covered by the First Amendment. The second attempt at dismissal reiterated Musk's First Amendment rights.

OpenAI countersued Musk in April, alleging harassment and his attempts to sabotage the company's transition to for-profit. The ChatGPT maker had asked a federal judge to stop Musk from any “further unlawful and unfair action.”

Musk filed a lawsuit against OpenAI — which he has a long history with — and CEO Sam Altman in August 2024, after dropping it initially, for allegedly putting profit ahead of benefiting humanity.

Musk's lawsuit also recently added Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), which has financially backed OpenAI, LinkedIn co-founder Reid Hoffman and Dee Templeton, a current Microsoft executive who previously worked at OpenAI, as co-defendants.

The 53-year-old Musk heads up OpenAI competitor xAI (along with Tesla (TSLA) and SpaceX (SPACE)), which is in the process of selling $300M to investors in a share sale. Investment firm Morgan Stanley is also in the process of selling $5B in debt for xAI to further strengthen its balance sheet. 

It was reported earlier this month that Morgan Stanley has told investors xAI is on track to hit $13B in adjusted earnings by 2029.

data.title: S&P rises, bonds rally after soft PPI, strong Treasury auction; Boeing weighs on Dow
data.description:
U.S. stocks on Thursday ticked higher, as traders weighed a positive producer inflation report and a strong Treasury bond auction against geopolitical risks and trade tensions. Bonds rallied on the inflation data. 

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +0.3% in afternoon trade, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.2%.

The Dow (DJI) seesawed and was last +0.2%. The blue-chip index was under pressure due to a -4.7% slide in component Boeing (BA). One of the planemaker's Dreamliner jets was involved in a crash in India that left hundreds dead.   

Before the opening bell, the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics reported a +0.1% M/M rise in the producer price index (PPI) for May vs. +0.2% expected. The core PPI also rose +0.1% vs. +0.3% consensus. The soft data came a day after a benign consumer price index (CPI) reading.    

Meanwhile, an update from the U.S. Department of Labor showed claims for insured employment - also known as continuing claims - for the week ending May 31 hitting its highest level since November 2021.

With inflation trending in the right direction and the labor market showing signs of cooling, market participants have increased their Federal Reserve rate cut expectations.  

“If the labor market data continues to cool and tariffs aren't meaningfully passed through to consumers, the Fed would certainly be warranted in cutting sooner and faster,” said Parker Ross, global chief economist at Arch Capital Group.

“I still expect the Fed to maintain a cautious stance. They are certainly hoping the labor softening sorts itself out once uncertainty is reduced around the trade war and will likely be waiting as long as possible to monitor any potential tariff impacts on inflation,” Ross added. 

U.S. Treasury yields tumbled after the PPI report, as traders snapped up bonds. The 10-year yield (US10Y) fell 6 basis points to 4.37%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 4 basis points to 3.92%.

Fixed-income securities were also in focus after a $22B 30-year bond auction traded through, indicating strong demand. 

Headlines on Thursday were also dominated by rising geopolitical risks in the Middle East, and a renewed tariff threat by U.S. President Donald Trump. 

The U.S. government on Wednesday ordered the departure of embassy staff from Baghdad after Iran threatened to attack U.S. bases if talks over its nuclear program fall through. JPMorgan said an attack on Iran could spike oil prices to $120 a barrel, which in turn could drive U.S. consumer prices up to 5%.      

On the trade front, Trump said he intended to send letters to trading partners in the next week or two setting unilateral tariff rates, ahead of a July 9 deadline to reimpose higher duties on dozens of countries.  

Also of note, shares of Chime Financial (CHYM) popped in their market debut on the Nasdaq, in one of the most closely watched fintech initial public offerings in recent years.

data.title: TransAlta takes off as Jefferies upgrades to Buy
data.description:
TransAlta (NYSE:TAC) +7.9% in Thursday's trading to its highest since January as Jefferies upgrades the Canadian power generation to Buy from Hold with a C$20 price target, raised from C$12, seeing more tangible evidence of durably higher AESO power prices following large load integration in 2027-28.

Analyst Julien Dumoulin-Smith said observed 2027 forward AESO pool prices have risen into the mid-C$60s, compared to his prior assumption of C$52.45, which due to TransAlta's (NYSE:TAC) extensive AB merchant exposure translates to nearly C$170M in FY 2027 free cash flow and C$6/share to his forecast stock price target.

The analyst said he previously was hesitant to underwrite rising AESO power prices, but indications from the admittedly illiquid at times AESO power price forwards and conversations with local stakeholders boost confidence to raise assumptions.

Dumoulin-Smith said his upgrade is not predicated on TransAlta's (NYSE:TAC) strategic direction, or on its ability to drive upside opportunities incremental to the existing portfolio, and the company's "harvesting higher AESO power prices at its AB merchant business is a singularly sufficient strategy for appreciation at this moment."

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Paris Air Show, FOMC meeting, Amazon event and triple-witching day
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

 

Monday - June 16

 

  • Volatility watch - New Fortress Energy (NFE) and CoreWeave (CRWV) have a higher level of implied volatility than normal based on options trading. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include SafeSpace Global (OTCPK:SSGC), Klotho Neurosciences (KLTO), and M3-Brigade Acquisition V. (MBAV). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include PG&E (PCG) and Copart (CPRT).
  • IPO watch - Cancer diagnostics firm Caris Life Science (CAI) and Forida-based insurance firm Slide Insurance Holdings (SLDE) are expected to price their IPO and begin trading.
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Lennar (LEN).
  • Dividend watch - Some companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include.....
  • All day - Amazon (AMZN) will hold its AWS re:Inforce conference in Philadelphia. The three-day event will provide the latest insights into securing workloads on AWS, including a keynote address from AWS Chief Information Security Officer Chris Betz. Shares of Amazon (AMZN) have rallied following the event in the past.
  • All day - The Paris Auto Show will begin. The event will present an opportunity for aerospace companies to unveil new technologies, announce strategic orders, and reshape investor sentiment. Boeing (BA), Airbus (OTCPK:EADSY), Embraer (ERJ), Honeywell (HON), Eve Holdings (EVEX), GE Aerospace (GE) are some of the companies participating.
  • All day - The three-day Marine Money Week event will begin in New York City. The event is widely regarded as the largest annual gathering of the most active dealmakers in global ship finance.

 

Tuesday - June 17

 

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Jabil (JBL) and La-Z-Boy (LZB).
  • All day - GE Aerospace (GE) Chairman and CEO Larry Culp, CFO Rahul Ghai and other members of the senior leadership team will provide updates on the businesses and outlook.
  • All day - Shareholders with Penn Entertainment (PENN) will vote on whether to support three independent director candidates nominated by activist investor HG Vora Capital Management or stick with the board's preferred slate.
  • All day - Merck KGaA (OTCPK:MKGAF) will showcase its portfolio at the Silicon Saxony Day event.
  • 8:30 a.m. The U.S. Census Bureau will release the May Retail Sales report.
  • 9:00 a.m. Equifax (EFX) will host an Investor Day event featuring presentations from the senior leadership team on the company's EFX2027 strategic priorities and investing in new products, data, analytics, and EFX.
  • 1:00 p.m. Marvell Technology (MRVL) will host a major webinar titled "The Future of Custom Silicon Technology for AI Infrastructure."

 

Wednesday - June 18

 

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Korn/Ferry (KFY) and Smith & Wesson Brands (SWBI).
  • All day - The three-day MWC Shanghai conference will begin. The event will showcase the latest advancements in mobile technology, including 5G, artificial intelligence, the Internet of Things, robotics, and more.
  • All day - Myomo(MYO) will host its first-ever Investor & Analyst Day event.
  • 11:00 a.m. Pure Storage (PSTG) CEO Charlie Giancarlo will give a keynote speech at the company's Pure//Accelerate event.
  • 2:00 p.m. The FOMC will make its interest rate policy announcement.
  • 2:30 p.m. Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell will hold a press conference.

 

Thursday - June 19

 

  • All day - The U.S. stock market is closed for observance of the Juneteenth federal holiday.

 

Friday - June 20

 

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Accenture (ACN), CarMax (KMX), Kroger (KR), and Darden Restaurants (DRI).
  • All day - The stock market could see extra volatility with triple witching day featuring the simultaneous expiration of stock options, stock index futures, and stock index options contracts.
  • All day - WTI crude July futures will expire. Crude oil futures have seen extra volatility over the last year on contract expiration dates.
  • 8:30 a.m. The Philadelphia Federal Index report will be released.
  • Postmarket - FTSE Russell will release the last update to the additions and deletions for the Russell indexes. Companies may be added or removed from the Russell 3000, Russell 1000, or Russell 2000 based on their market capitalization and other eligibility.
